chunk_summary: This section discusses micronutrient supplementation and infection prevention for children with moderate wasting, emphasizing integration with public health programs like Anaemia Mukt Bharat, deworming, and Vitamin A prophylaxis, alongside infection prevention strategies.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of public health programs in addressing micronutrient deficiencies in children with moderate wasting.
Learn the prophylactic schedules for iron-folic acid, Vitamin A, and deworming in children.
Recognize the importance of infection prevention measures in managing moderate wasting.
Apply WHO guidelines for zinc supplementation during acute diarrhoea.
key_facts: Anaemia Mukt Bharat provides IFA syrup (20 mg iron, 100 mcg folic acid) twice weekly for children aged 6–59 months.
Albendazole is given as a single 200 mg dose for ages 1–2 years and 400 mg for ages 2–19 years under National Deworming Day.
Vitamin A prophylaxis includes 100,000 IU at 9 months and 200,000 IU every 6 months up to 5 years.
Zinc supplementation for acute diarrhoea: 20 mg daily for 10–14 days (10 mg daily if under 6 months).
In children with clinical anaemia, treatment should follow national anaemia pathways rather than relying on prophylaxis alone.
